sentence_reviews.text: Parallel inference partitions tensors, layers, sequence positions, requests, or experts across multiple devices and coordinates the required communication.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Megatron-LM directly supports tensor and pipeline model parallelism for large language models, but not every listed partitioning mode such as requests, sequence positions, or experts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Definition is broadly plausible but should add sources for sequence/request/expert parallel inference or narrow to model parallelism.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference is to connect Model servers & inference runtimes, Distributed systems & networking to downstream capabilities including Large language models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The sources support model parallelism and broad distributed systems context, but the downstream atlas linkage is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible as graph synthesis; not directly stated by the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Megatron-LM uses model parallelism to train multi-billion-parameter language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source is training-focused, but supports the broader role of parallelism as a substrate for large language models.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Model servers & inference runtimes
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Megatron-LM supports model-parallel execution concepts, but not model-serving integration specifically.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs an inference-serving source for the exact example.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Model servers & inference runtimes, Distributed systems & networking.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Distributed systems sources support operational failure concerns generally; the full list and dependency sentence are atlas-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Concurrency and cost are plausible; security, versioning, observability, and numerical behavior need more direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference turns Model servers & inference runtimes into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The source supports model parallelism for scaling large models, not the model-serving operationalization wording.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core relationship is plausible; exact rationale is graph-template wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference turns Distributed systems & networking into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Model parallelism requires multi-device communication; DDIA supports distributed systems concerns broadly.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship type and core strength are defensible, though the integrity-preserving phrasing is overbroad.

sentence_reviews.text: Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference turns Mixture-of-experts models into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: MoE and model-parallel sources jointly support expert and scaling concepts, but not this exact operationalization claim.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is plausible; direct evidence for inference-specific expert parallelism would improve it.

sentence_reviews.text: Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference turns Checkpoint, adapter & expert composition into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support adapters/model composition and model parallelism separately; they do not directly establish this dependency.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependency may be true for serving composed checkpoints, but the cited evidence is indirect.

edge_reviews.notes: Parallel inference is a serving scalability mechanism, but direct serving-runtime evidence is missing.
Multi-device partitioning and communication make distributed systems a core dependency.
Expert parallelism is relevant to MoE scaling, but this packet lacks a direct expert-parallel inference source.
The relationship is plausible in composed model serving but not established by the cited sources.
